title: B6. Binary Representation Minimization
label: main
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B6-binary-representation-minimization
Title: B6. Binary Representation Minimization

Question:
For each n, let k(n) be the number of ones in the binary representation of 2023*n.
Find the minimum k(n).

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: 3
JUSTIFICATION: The number 2023 in binary has 7 ones (2023 = 11111100111₂). Multiplying by n shifts and adds copies of 2023’s binary pattern. The minimal number of ones in 2023*n occurs when n is a power of two, shifting 2023’s bits without adding new ones. Thus, the minimal k(n) equals the number of ones in 2023 itself, which is 7.
